title: Smoky Creamy Brazilian Fish Stew
description: A comforting, smoky and creamy fish stew that is sugar‑free and perfect for a sick day, served in scallop shells for an elegant touch.
instructions: Preheat a skillet over medium heat and lightly oil it to prevent sticking.
Season cod fillets with salt and pepper, then sear them in the skillet until golden brown on both sides, about 2 minutes per side.
Reduce heat to low, pour coconut milk over the fillets, and let it simmer gently for 5 minutes.
Stir in fish sauce nam pla, lime juice, minced garlic, minced ginger, and smoked paprika, allowing the flavors to meld for another 3 minutes.
Transfer the fillets and broth into cleaned scallop shells, filling each shell almost to the top.
Garnish each shell with a drizzle of the glaze mixture, made by simmering the remaining sauce with an extra pinch of smoked paprika until slightly thickened.
Cover the scallop shells with foil and steam them on low heat for 5 minutes to finish cooking the fish.
Remove from heat, let rest for 2 minutes, then serve the smoky creamy stew in the scallop shells with fresh lime wedges on the side.
